title: Kashi
content:

{{Quote|The Kashi Guardians of Breath still persist, despite the ancient destruction of their homeworld by a supernova.|Restelly Quist, ''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force''|<ref name="JP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>}}
'''Kashi''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Phelleem sector/Legends|Phelleem sector]]. At one point [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the ancient, isolationist [[Kashi Mer]] civilization, it was governed by the [[Kashi Mer Dynasty]], whose influence also extended outside the [[Kashi system]]. The [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-user]] organization known as the [[Guardians of the Breath]] played an important role in the Kashi Mer religion.

Around [[24,500 BBY]], the student of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] [[Reda Jalooz]] stole from Kashi a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Kashi Mer talisman|talisman]] that served as the symbol of the local [[Monarchy/Legends|monarchy]]. Jalooz soon returned the [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifact]] to the planet; however, at that point Kashi's [[Star/Legends|sun]] suddenly and inexplicably exploded in a [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]], destroying the entire [[Star system/Legends|star system]]. As a result, the Kashi Mer culture largely went extinct. [[Historian/Legends|Historians]] later attributed the destruction of Kashi to the Kashi Mer talisman, which had survived the disaster.

==Description==
Kashi was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that at one point [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the primary<ref name="CSWE KashiMer">{{CSWECite|II|201|Kashi Mer}}</ref> [[Star/Legends|star]] of the [[Kashi system]],<ref name="RelicReprint" /> a part of the [[Phelleem sector/Legends|Phelleem sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Sector" />

==History==
===Destruction of a star system===
{{Quote|I had the Kashi Mer talisman bequeathed to me by Xendor, the symbol of his sundered royal house ''[…]''<nowiki />|Filed selections from the debriefing of [[Arden Lyn]]|<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>}}
[[File:KashiMerTalisman-Journal6.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Kashi Mer talisman was theorized to have caused the destruction of Kashi.]]
By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Constitution Conference|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="RelicReprint" /> in [[25,053 BBY]],<ref name="SWTOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> the [[Kashi Mer]] culture had existed on Kashi for [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]]. The student of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] [[Reda Jalooz]] stole a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]<ref name="RelicReprint" /> [[Kashi Mer talisman|talisman]]<ref name="Gamer5">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref> that was an heirloom<ref name="RelicReprint" /> and symbol of<ref name="EGTW" /> the [[Kashi Mer Dynasty]],<ref name="CSWE KashiMer" /> the [[Monarchy/Legends|monarchy]] of Kashi,<ref name="RelicReprint" /> at some point by [[24,500 BBY]].<ref name="24500BBY">According to {{JournalCite|6|Relic (article)}}, [[Reda Jalooz]] stole the [[Kashi Mer talisman]] several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] prior to the destruction of the [[Kashi system]], which occurred at some point by [[24,500 BBY]] per {{KashiDestructionDate}}.</ref> Several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, after having realized [[Gender/Legends|her]] misdeed, Jalooz returned the [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifact]] to the planet in order to ask forgiveness from the Kashi Mer.<ref name="RelicReprint" />

However, soon after Jalooz arrived on Kashi's surface, the Kashi [[Star system/Legends|system's]] sun suddenly exploded in a [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]], destroying Kashi<ref name="RelicReprint" /> and the rest of the system.<ref name="CSWE KashiMer" /> Later [[Historian/Legends|historians]] theorized that<ref name="CSWE RedaJalooz">{{CSWECite|II|136|Jalooz, Reda}}</ref> the talisman could have been the cause of the catastrophe,<ref name="ImperialWarlords">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> although the artifact itself survived the supernova,<ref name="RelicReprint" /> eventually falling into the hands of the [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] Kashi Mer [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Xendor]].<ref name="EGTW" /> By [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], the [[Orixon Nebula/Legends|Orixon Nebula]] had formed out of the former Kashi system.<ref name="TEA" />

===Legacy===
[[File:Eleaor_Propulsion.svg|thumb|right|300px|Eleaor Propulsion was founded by the Kashi noble Krispus Eleaor.]]
Although the Kashi Mer culture was rendered extinct by Kashi's destruction,<ref name="CSWE KashiMer" /> it was survived by two entities, [[Eleaor Propulsion]] and the [[Guardians of the Breath]]. The former was a vehicle manufacturing company that was founded by the Kashi [[Noble/Legends|aristocrat]] [[Krispus Eleaor]], was originally based in the Kashi system,<ref name="TotJC">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref> and survived up until around [[3956 BBY]].<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> The latter was the dominant Kashi Mer tradition of Force-users,<ref name="TotJC" /> which hailed from Kashi and remained active, albeit scattered,<ref name="JP" /> by [[990 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="990 BBY">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/12/endnotes-for-star-wars-jedi-path-part-1.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Jedi Path (part 1 of 4)|quote=Okay, so we've established that this particular copy of the [[Book/Legends|book]] dates from [[115 BBY]]. But the content inside is [...] dating from the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[990 BBY]].}}</ref> a section of the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainee]] [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'' [[Author/Legends|authored]] by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Chief Librarian/Legends|Chief Librarian]] [[Restelly Quist/Legends|Restelly Quist]] mentioned both the Guardians of the Breath and the fate of their homeworld.<ref name="JP" />

Many years prior to<ref name="RelicReprint" /> [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="9 ABY">{{JournalCite|6|Relic (article)}} is set during the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] period of recuperation immediately following the [[Thrawn campaign]] and, per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', therefore also before the [[Operation Shadow Hand|onslaught]] of the returned [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. The latter work dates those two events to [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] and [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], respectively, meaning "Relic" must be set around 9 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]].</ref> the school [[Teacher/Legends|teachers]] of a group of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] agents taught them of the destruction of Kashi. By that year, the cause of the disaster remained unexplained, although the events immediately leading up to it were noted in a [[Scandoc/Legends|scandoc]] that formed a part of the collection of documents on ancient cultures gathered by the [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologist]] [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Oron]].<ref name="RelicReprint" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Kashi Mer}}
{{Quote|At Ossus I met Xendor, the man of my vision. He was a Kashi Mer exile, a master of the Breath, as his people called the Force.|Filed selections from the debriefing of Arden Lyn|<ref name="EGTW" />}}
[[File:GuardianOfTheBreath-TheJediPath.png|thumb|left|300px|The Guardians of the Breath originated on Kashi.]]
Kashi was the homeworld of the Kashi Mer culture,<ref name="RelicReprint" /> members of which were also known as the Kashi. The Kashi Mer had access to [[Starship/Legends|starships]] capable of interstellar travel, and as a result the influence of their culture,<ref name="TotJC" /> which was ruled by the monarchy<ref name="RelicReprint" /> known as the Kashi Mer Dynasty,<ref name="CSWE KashiMer" /> spread beyond the Kashi system. Despite that, the Kashi Mer remained isolationist, going so far as to refusing to speak to outsiders.<ref name="TotJC" />

The culture produced exotic [[Carpet/Legends|carpets]],<ref name="RelicReprint" /> and its religion included belief in the dual triumvirate of [[Deity/Legends|deities]] known as [[Niman]]<ref name="TDFS4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}}</ref> as well as the Force, which was known to the Kashi Mer as "the Breath." The hierarchical organization known as the Guardians of the Breath kept the lore of the Breath as well as the methods for using it. They also utilized [[Force vision/Legends|Force visions]] to inform the Kashi Mer civilization as a whole of the path it should follow. Ever year, tens of thousands of Kashi Mer applied for membership among the Guardians, although extremely few of the applicants ever advanced far in the organization.<ref name="TotJC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Relic-Journal6.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Kashi was introduced in the roleplaying adventure "Relic."]]
Kashi was first mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Relic (article)|Relic]]," which was authored by [[George R. Strayton]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[May]] [[1995]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 6|sixth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine.<ref name="Journal6">{{JournalCite|6|Relic (article)}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Kashi system, and therefore Kashi, in grid square S-7.<ref name="TEA" />

Both Strayton's [[1996]] roleplaying sourcebook ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''<ref name="TotJC" /> and [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' introduced several contradictions regarding the dating of Kashi's destruction.<ref name="CSWE RedaJalooz" /> The former work, set approximately 4,000 years before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], presents information on the Kashi system as if it still existed at that time,<ref name="TotJC" /> despite "Relic" stating that the system had been destroyed shortly after the formation of the Galactic Republic<ref name="Journal6" />—an event that had already been established by 1996 to have occurred around twenty-five millennia before the events of ''A New Hope''.<ref name="Guide2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> This article assumes that the information from the ''Tales of the Jedi Companion'' regarding the Kashi system instead applies to the time period preceding its destruction.

''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' entry for Reda Jalooz claims that she stole the Kashi Mer talisman<ref name="CSWE RedaJalooz" />—which, per "Relic," was followed by Kashi's destruction mere months later<ref name="RelicReprint" />—before the Republic's foundation. The entry also claims that the artifact resurfaced in the [[Corva sector/Legends|Corva sector]]<ref name="CSWE RedaJalooz" />—an event that "Relic" places shortly after the Thrawn campaign<ref name="RelicReprint" />—only "a millennium" later.<ref name="CSWE RedaJalooz" /> This comes into direct conflict with ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' itself, which dates the establishment of the Republic to [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]] and the Thrawn campaign to 9 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]].<ref name="CSWE Timeline">{{CSWECite|I|xi-xv|A Timeline of Galactic Events}}</ref> Furthermore, Jalooz's entry is contradicted by the entry for the Kashi Mer, which states that, around the time of the [[First Great Schism]]<ref name="CSWE KashiMer" />—which is dated by the book to 24,500 BBY<ref name="CSWE Timeline" />—the culture had not yet gone extinct as a result of the destruction of Kashi.<ref name="CSWE KashiMer" /> This article therefore assumes that the destruction of Kashi occurred at some point either during the First Great Schism or shortly before that event.
